task_completion.description: Open the dwanko_run.tnz scene in OpenToonz
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application is open and the title bar, Xsheet, or timeline clearly shows 'dwanko_run' loaded.

task_completion.description: Apply a Chromatic Aberration or RGB Shift effect to the scene
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FX Schematic window is open and shows an 'RGB Shift' (or similar chromatic aberration) node added and connected to the scene graph/Xsheet.

task_completion.description: Shift the Red channel horizontally to the right
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FX Settings dialog for the RGB Shift effect is visible, showing the Red channel's horizontal (X) offset value increased (positive) relative to the Green and Blue channels.

task_completion.description: Set the render output path and format
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Output Settings window is open with the 'Save In' path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/aberration/' (or similar valid relative path) and the file format set to 'png'.

task_completion.description: Render at least 12 frames of the sequence
task_completion.visual_evidence: A render progress bar showing frames 1 to 12 being processed, or a file manager/terminal window showing at least 12 PNG files generated in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/aberration/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to apply the effect and render the scene
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating OpenToonz menus, the FX schematic, and render settings rather than bypassing the software.

integrity.description: The agent did not use external scripts or terminal tools to fake the effect
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal commands using tools like ImageMagick, FFmpeg, or Python scripts to apply the RGB shift directly to image files.

integrity.description: The agent actually rendered the files from OpenToonz
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen initiating the render process from within OpenToonz (e.g., clicking 'Render'), rather than copying or downloading pre-existing images into the output folder.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target scene is '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz'. The output directory must be exactly '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/aberration' and contain at least 12 PNG frames. The Red channel must be shifted horizontally to the right by at least 5 pixels relative to the Green and Blue channels.
